stories.title: Ukraine war briefing: Russia bans diesel exports as refinery attacks ⁠trigger gas shortages and price spikes
stories.summary: Moscow moves to support domestic fuel market as drivers ⁠face hours-long lines amid intensifying Ukrainian strikes. What we know on day 1,597 Russia introduced a ban on diesel exports on Wednesday as part of a raft of measures to support the domestic fuel market after systematic Ukrainian drone attacks on oil refineries ⁠triggered gasoline shortages and price spikes. Drivers ⁠in many regions are ​facing hours-long lines to refuel, as intensifying Ukrainian strikes on Russian energy infrastructur

stories.title: Le Pen’s strategy to win French presidency: Bring Bardella everywhere
stories.summary: PARIS — Marine Le Pen’s first campaign stop in western France on Wednesday revealed a major clue on how she plans to win over voters long skeptical of the far right: Keep her charming, 30-year-old protégé attached at the hip. Le Pen’s thinking is that Jordan Bardella’s youth appeal, comms skills and history of outreach to the business elite can help widen the appeal of their party, the National Rally, and compensate for her weaknesses. After trying — and failing — three times to reach the Elysée

stories.title: The most shameless pitches for a job in Andy Burnham’s team — ranked
stories.summary: LONDON — It’s like The X Factor never went away.  Senior Labour politicians are hitting the airwaves and social media extolling the virtues of Andy Burnham — and themselves.  As the man pretty much nailed on to be Britain’s next prime minister considers who he will put in his Cabinet, POLITICO runs through the most brazen pitches for power from MPs once loyal to Keir Starmer. Wes Streeting  The ex-health secretary once favored a contest rather than coronation to replace Starmer —

stories.title: The Guardian view on Marine Le Pen’s candidacy: a dangerous gamble | Editorial
stories.summary: The far-right leader has reached for the Trumpian playbook in plotting a possible path to the Élysée. The consequences are alarmingly unpredictable Back in 2013, when a Socialist minister was accused (and eventually convicted) of tax fraud, the righteous fury of Marine Le Pen knew no bounds. Any politician found guilty of financial misconduct, she fulminated , should be ineligible for office for the rest of their lives. That was very much then. Although a court of appeal on Tuesday upheld h

stories.title: Democrats accuse Graham Platner of trying to put ‘thumb on the scale’ of process to replace him
stories.summary: Frustration mounts that Maine nominee has not withdrawn over sexual assault claim as Trump says Platner ‘in a bind’ Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email Graham Platner, the embattled Democratic nominee for US Senate in Maine, has been accused of trying to influence the process of picking his replacement as frustration mounts that he has not yet withdrawn from the race. Platner has publicly said he is “taking the time to reflect on the best path forward” after an allegation of sexual

stories.title: Hungary public media reform begins with black screen apology
stories.summary: People tuning in to public service media in Hungary on Tuesday were greeted by a black screen and an apology. Peter Magyar has begun dismantling one of Viktor Orban's most potent tools of power.

stories.title: Nigeria's president demands to know how fake agency was allegedly set up in his office
stories.summary: Adeniyi Adeyemi allegedly forged a letter of appointment and secured funding for an agency the presidency knew nothing about.

stories.title: Van valami furcsa és megmagyarázhatatlan a magyar olajimportban
stories.summary: Három hónapig nem működött a Barátság kőolajvezeték, és bár a Mol a hírek szerint jó pár tengeri olajszállítmányt megszervezett a kiesés pótlására, a statisztikákban ennek nincs semmi nyoma.

stories.title: Ha egymás után 26-szor fekete jött ki a ruletten, a pirosra tenne a következő gurításnál?
stories.summary: 1913-ban Monte-Carlóban sokan így tettek, és milliókat veszítettek vele. Minden idők legbizarrabb rulettjátszmája jól mutatja, hogy működik az emberi agy, és mi az a szerencsejátékos tévedése.

stories.title: Tiszás újoncok a parlamentben: nem gombnyomogató embernek mentek oda, de a sok ülés próbára teszi őket
stories.summary: A Tisza országgyűlési képviselői közül volt, aki a tanári pályát hagyta ott a képviselőségért, más a pénzügyi világból vagy a turizmusból jött. Pár hét alatt kellett beletanulniuk a parlamenti munkába. A maratoni plenáris üléseket még szokniuk kell, a frakcióüléseken vannak viták, ellenvetések, Magyar Péter pedig kifejezetten szereti, ha „challengelik”.

stories.title: Olcsón megúszta Elon Musk: 1,5 millió dollárra büntették, miután 150 milliót nyert a Twitter-vásárlás körüli szabálytalanságokon
stories.summary: Egyezséget kötött az amerikai tőzsdefelügyelettel. A bíróság ugyan aggályosnak nevezte, mégis jóváhagyta a megállapodást.

stories.title: Úgy bánt a Fidesz az országgal, mint egy játszani akaró kutyával
stories.summary: Önkényt kiált a Fidesz, pedig ha volt önkényes hatalomgyakorlás Magyarországon, azt éppen ők valósították meg. Élő műsorban frissítettük fel olvasóink emlékezetét Orbán Viktor kormányzásának önkényes lépéseiről, de arról is beszéltünk, milyen kritikák vethetőek fel a Tisza által kezdeményezett 17. Alaptörvény-módosításról.

stories.title: Rév Marcellt Emmy-díjra jelölték az Eufória fináléjáért
stories.summary: A magyar operatőr egyszer már elnyerte a legrangosabb amerikai tévés díjat, akkor is az Eufória miatt ismerték el.

stories.title: A Barcelona volt védője lett a mexikói válogatott új kapitánya, az egyiptomi sikeredző hosszabbított
stories.summary: Rafael Márquez veszi át Javier Aguirre helyét. Hoszám Haszan a 2030-as vb-ig írt alá új szerződést.

stories.title: Hegedűs Zsolt feltáncolta magát a New York Times legstílusosabb emberek listájára
stories.summary: A magyar egészségügyi miniszter lazasága a lap szerint „ritka és ragadós”, így kerülhetett a díszes társaságba.

stories.title: Elsőre csak gaz látszott, de kiderült: megvannak a facsemeték, amikből majd véderdő lesz a szegedi BYD-gyár mellett
stories.summary: Hivatalosan 7200 facsemetét ültettek el, az is felmerült, hogy beszántották őket, de ez nem igazolódott. Előkerültek a fák a gaz és a búza között.

stories.title: Magyar Péter arról írt, Áder János adófizetői pénzből nyaralt Amerikában, a volt köztársasági elnök szerint ez hazugság
stories.summary: A miniszterelnök szerint közpénzből fizették az Áder család belépőit is Disneylandbe, Áder szerint hivatalos úton vettek részt, a lányuk utazási költségeit pedig maguk fizették.

stories.title: Ezek voltak a propaganda mélypontjai az M1-en
stories.summary: A köztévé képernyőjén az elmúlt években számos alkalommal tiporták földbe az újságírás elemi szakmai és etikai szabályait. Hírhamisítás, nyakló nélkül sulykolt kormányzati üzenetek híreknek álcázva, nettó háborús rémisztgetés, orosz minta, totális kontroll, ahol még a Reuters hírét is sikerült úgy eltorzítani, hogy Magyar Péter robbantja ki a harmadik világháborút.

stories.title: Reagált Pósfai Gábor belügyminiszter Szakács István megafonos propagandista szerda reggeli előállítására
stories.summary: Korábban Orbán Viktor is posztolt a témáról.

stories.title: A Pénzügyminisztérium talált 400 milliárd forint kiadást, ami nem szerepelt Nagy Mártonék idei költségvetésében
stories.summary: Kármán Andrásék úgy számolnak, hogy ha az Orbán-kormány maradt volna hatalmon, idén 8,3 százalékos lenne a költségvetési hiány.

stories.title: Sikeres volt a NATO-csúcs Magyar Péter szerint, aki most pár napot még marad nyaralni Törökországban
stories.summary: A miniszterelnök a kormánya ellen szerveződő fideszes tüntetésről is beszélt friss videójában.

stories.title: Irán beletenyerelt a NATO-csúcsba, Trump újra összeveszett az európai szövetségesekkel
stories.summary: Ingatagnak, de nyugodtnak ígérkezett az ankarai csúcstalálkozó. Aztán az iráni tűzszünet összeomlása felszínre hozta a transzatlanti feszültséget, mert az európaiak nem akarnak részt venni a katonai rendezésben. Az amerikai elnök a kereskedelem felfüggesztését is elrendelte a leginkább elutasító Spanyolországgal szemben.

stories.title: Kielce pogrom in Poland: 80th anniversary of 1946 massacre
stories.summary: Just over a year after World War II ended, a mob in the Polish city of Kielce killed around 40 Jews, some of them Holocaust survivors. A lie about ritual child murder escalated the violence.

stories.title: Trump to ask US supreme court to reconsider birthright citizenship ruling
stories.summary: Request for rehearing comes after Fox News report of Texas hospital advertising maternity services in Mexico Donald Trump said on Wednesday he would ask the US supreme court to reconsider its ruling that the 14th amendment to the US constitution guarantees birthright citizenship in light of what he described as shocking new evidence: a hospital in Texas advertising its services to expectant mothers in Mexico on a pair of billboards. “Signs and Billboards are being put up all over our Southern Bo

stories.title: Ceasefire plunged into greater uncertainty amid fresh US strikes in Iran – as it happened
stories.summary: US president says new strikes are ‘retribution’ after ships hit in strait of Hormuz; Iranian state media report explosions in several locations US carries out another wave of strikes on Iran Wednesday’s strikes against Iran expected to be ​greater in number than those carried out on Tuesday, an unnamed U.S. official told Reuters. Sahar, a 34-year-old teacher in Tehran who uses only one name to protect her identity, said the latest attacks have left families worried about new strikes on the resid

stories.title: US launches strikes on Iran for a second day after Trump says agreement to end the war is ‘over’
stories.summary: Attacks on three locations across Iran came after three tankers in the strait of Hormuz were targeted on Tuesday The US military carried out strikes on Iran for a second day, hours after president Donald Trump said that an interim agreement to end the war was “over” . Late on Wednesday Iranian state media reported explosions in the port city of Bandar Abbas in the strait of Hormuz; in Sirik, another southern coastal city; and the south-western Bushehr province, home to Iran’s nuclear-power-plant

stories.title: Iran reports casualties and infrastructure damage after US strikes as tensions in the Gulf rise
stories.summary: Iran’s Foreign Ministry said the latest wave of US strikes constituted “a blatant war crime" and condemned what it called “aggressive actions” by the US military. Tehran responded with strikes of its own towards Gulf countries.

stories.title: Burnham eyes a casual version of the special relationship
stories.summary: LONDON — Efforts to preserve a close White House relationship dominated Keir Starmer’s premiership and ultimately helped doom him. His anticipated successor Andy Burnham’s intentions on the world stage are emerging gradually. The signs so far suggest he will neither rush to love-bomb Trump nor seek to antagonize him, taking a cooler approach than Starmer. The first obvious opportunity for Burnham to visit Washington will be the U.N. General Assembly in September, but he may opt to skip it in lin

stories.title: Europe’s AI moment: Four imperatives for business leaders
stories.summary: Business in the age of artificial intelligence (AI) moves with dizzying speed. More powerful models launch regularly, bringing new opportunities and risks. Fresh use cases emerge daily, increasingly leaning on the orchestration power of agentic AI. Innovation boundaries recede as the cost of inference declines and robotics accelerates. It’s as if we’re permanently on fast forward. I reflected on the speed of change with Samantha Garretto at Politico recently . A year on from our first meeting, t

stories.title: ‘They said: wear angelic white’: British women who accused US airman of rape tell of American military trial
stories.summary: Two women who alleged they were raped by Tyrion Davis in Suffolk had to testify at an invasive court martial on a US base Minutes after fleeing the home of an American airman, Rebecca called 999 in tears to report that he had raped her. She recalls vomiting at a police station in Suffolk as she described being repeatedly and violently attacked. Officers took her to a sexual assault referral centre for an intimate examination. There, a nurse measured and photographed her injuries, including bruis

stories.title: Merz: Germany agreed to buy Tomahawk missiles at NATO summit
stories.summary: Chancellor Friedrich Merz has informed the Bundestag parliament of a deal to buy US Tomahawk cruise missiles and station them in Germany. He said this closed a "important strategic ‌gap ​in our defense."

stories.title: UK must regulate lobbying after Mandelson scandal, ethics watchdog tells Burnham
stories.summary: LONDON — Andy Burnham faces demands to quickly bring in a sweeping redesign of the U.K.’s lobbying rules following a major review prompted by the Peter Mandelson scandal. The U.K. government’s independent Ethics and Integrity Commission (EIC) said in a report Thursday that new laws are necessary to fix inadequacies in Westminster’s lobbying and government transparency systems. It will almost certainly fall to Burnham — who is expected to become the next U.K. prime minister on J

stories.title: Trump switches back to flying on older Air Force One for England trip instead of new Qatari jet
stories.summary: US president unexpectedly changed plans for flight to England after using Qatari-gifted aircraft to travel to Turkey Donald Trump flew from Turkey to England onboard the older Air Force One on Wednesday, rather than the new, Qatari-gifted aircraft he used to travel to the Nato summit in Ankara, an unexpected change in plans that prompted questions about security fears. Trump later boarded the new plane at Mildenhall air force base in the United Kingdom for the trip back to Washington. Continue r

stories.title: Populism unites Le Pen and Farage. But she is a step closer to power
stories.summary: Marine Le Pen is a convicted criminal. But now she’s running for office and there is still no credible candidate to oppose her As the judge read out the verdict in Marine Le Pen’s appeal trial for embezzlement, the same conversation was playing out in living rooms and WhatsApp groups across France. What? Does this mean she can run for the Élysée after all? But what about the prison sentence? And the electronic tag (which Le Pen had promised she would not campaign wearing)? stories.title: Reform activists urged to switch focus from Manchester to Farage’s Clacton contest
stories.summary: Exclusive: Party hoped to mount serious challenge in mayoral race but attention has been diverted to Essex UK politics live – latest updates Reform UK activists have been urged to divert from the Greater Manchester mayoral byelection to support Nigel Farage’s “fake” contest 250 miles away in Clacton. A WhatsApp message shared with party members in northwest England said: “The message could not be more clear. We now need all of our fantastic activists, branch officers and councillors to come and

stories.title: Burnham calls for ‘more open’ public debate about defence spending – UK politics live
stories.summary: Next Labour leader, who is set to be elected unopposed, outlines foreign policy principles largely in line with Starmer Burnham promises he will not use party discipline to stifle debate There is an urgent question in the Commons at 10.30am. Priti Patel, the shadow foreign secretary, has asked for a statement about the “Iran’s violation of the ceasefire”. And there will be two ministerial statements after business questions. stories.title: Through the teargas, I saw something missing from German politics for too long: hope | Scott Roxborough
stories.summary: I joined thousands of people blockading the AfD’s Erfurt congress. A civil disobedience movement is showing how to beat the far right At 5am on Saturday morning, I found myself jogging across a field with a few hundred strangers, on my way to block a highway. We were just outside the east German city of Erfurt, one of several groups setting up roadblocks to try to stop delegates from reaching the far-right Alternative für Deutschland (AfD) party conference. We set up facing a row of police in ri

stories.title: US and Iran threaten return to war after fiercest exchange of fire since truce
stories.summary: Ceasefire appears close to collapse as two militaries target each other after after Iran strikes three commercial vessels Iran and the US have threatened to reignite their conflict after the most extensive exchange of fire since an interim deal was signed last month. On a chaotic day at the Nato summit in Ankara, Donald Trump said the US would probably hit the country again on Wednesday evening and renewed his threat to take control of the strategic Kharg Island in the strait of Hormuz - a move

stories.title: Marine Le Pen ‘wants to talk politics’, but can she drown out the legal noise?
stories.summary: The French far-right leader’s presidential campaign is clouded in uncertainty under the shadow of an embezzlement conviction Marine Le Pen ’s decision to run for French president in 2027, despite her legal woes, has drawn comparisons from her opponents to Donald Trump. Just as the US president felt his voter base cared little about legal investigations against him, the French far-right leader shrugged off the leftwing protesters who shouted “criminal!” as she launched her presidential campaign a

stories.title: Sabre-rattling to ‘tremendous love’: erratic Trump dominates final hours of Nato summit
stories.summary: Alliance leaders, who had feared the worst, will hail US president’s renewed support for article 5 as key victory An erratic and at times irascible Donald Trump has said he felt a “tremendous love” from western leaders at the Nato summit, only hours after lambasting them over their defence spending and not helping the US in attacking Iran. The US president’s mixed messaging dominated the final hours of the two-day gathering in Ankara, Turkey, beginning with him publicly calling Iran’s leadership

stories.title: South Korea's top court upholds ex-president Yoon's 7-year sentence
stories.summary: The court dismissed Yoon's appeals, saying there ​was no misunderstanding of any legal interpretations in the rulings.

stories.title: Iran war: Trump calls second set of US strikes 'retribution'
stories.summary: The US launched fresh strikes on Iran overnight, the second wave of attacks in 24 hours after President Trump declared a ceasefire to be over. Meanwhile, Iran is preparing to bury slain leader Khamenei. DW has more.

stories.title: Germany: June heat wave linked to 5,000 excess deaths
stories.summary: Several other European Union countries reported thousands of additional deaths as June 2026 officially became the hottest on record for bloc.

stories.title: Germany news: Man jailed over killing of train conductor
stories.summary: A verdict has been delivered in a trial that drew national attention and raised concern about train staff safety. Meanwhile, VW bosses are eyeing huge restructuring, with unions vowing to fight any closures. DW has more.

stories.title: Renewed US-Iran war would hit Gulf countries hard
stories.summary: After US strikes on Iran, Iran targeted US military sites in Bahrain and Kuwait. Even if Iran isn't targeting the governments or populations of its neighbors, Gulf countries have much to lose as fighting escalates.

stories.title: Watch: Why is there a 'black cloud' over unity at the Nato summit?
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump has used the Nato summit to criticise the lack of support for his war on Iran, and revive his interest in taking over Greenland.

stories.title: Graham Platner ends Maine Senate campaign after sexual assault allegation
stories.summary: Democratic nominee, dogged by controversy since entering contest, says ‘for the movement to continue, it can’t be me’ Woman accuses Maine Senate candidate Graham Platner of sexual assault Graham Platner, the Democratic nominee for US Senate in Maine, is suspending his campaign following sexual assault allegations. Platner announced his decision in an 11-minute video posted to social media on Wednesday evening, in which he angrily accused the Democratic establishment and corporate media of “using

stories.title: Teatime in Tbilisi: Georgia’s Soviet-era plantations brew up a renaissance
stories.summary: Georgia’s tea industry collapsed alongside the Soviet Union but is now reaching a luxury market Rainclouds shroud the Caucasus mountains as the day’s harvest begins on a rural estate in western Georgia. A tea picker moves quickly between bushes with confidence, her hands plucking only the greenest, most recent growth on each plant. When Pati began picking tea leaves as a teenager this was a collective farm in the Soviet Union – following its collapse it was abandoned and the bushes swallowed by

stories.title: Western Europe records hottest-ever June as heatwaves intensify
stories.summary: Temperatures across ravaged region 3C above average as scientists warn of risks for people, ecosystems and infrastructure Record wildfires in Europe show failure to adapt carries a mounting cost Western Europe has been scorched by its hottest June on record, scientists have said, as the UK enters its third heatwave of the year and wildfires ravage France and Spain. Inflamed by carbon pollution , the deadly June heatwave helped push surface air temperatures for the region 3.06C above their averag

stories.title: Great Britain’s grid operator issues another warning over power supplies in heatwave
stories.summary: Neso asks for extra supplies from electricity generators to cope with added demand on Thursday night Great Britain’s energy system operator has warned that “extreme temperatures” could hit power supplies on Thursday night, as the UK entered its third heatwave of the year. The National Energy System Operator (Neso) issued a notice overnight asking for extra supplies from power generators to cope with the added demand from households turning on fans and air conditioners to cope with the high tempe

stories.title: Venomous snakes escape breeding farms in southern China during flooding
stories.summary: Local media in Hengzhou report king ratsnakes and cobras among hundreds in flood waters caused by typhoon Maysak Hundreds of snakes, including cobras, have escaped from flooded breeding farms in southern China as severe storms continue to batter parts of the country. State media reported that a snake farm in Hengzhou, in Guangxi Zhuang autonomous region, was hit by flood waters after days of heavy rainfall caused by typhoon Maysak , prompting warnings for nearby residents. Continue reading...

stories.title: Trump says Iran truce is ‘over’ as US hits 170 targets over two nights – Middle East crisis live
stories.summary: Interim truce looks increasingly precarious as Iran carried out retaliatory strikes on US bases in Bahrain, Kuwait and Qatar Massive crowds have begun marching through streets of Mashhad in north-east Iran where the slain supreme leader Ali Khamenei will be buried later today. It follows a week of funeral processions around Iran and Iraq that has coincided with the fresh bout of fighting with the US. Continue reading...

stories.title: ‘A lot of art in Ireland was made by one type of man’: Richard Malone on taking his colourful fabric creations to the EU Council
stories.summary: As his bold yet delicate sculptures fill the glass buildings of Brussels for Ireland’s turn at the EU presidency, the Wexford artist discusses working with Björk, his decorator father – and one noisy horse ‘Just so you know,” says Richard Malone before we begin talking, “if you hear any neighing, it’s not me!” The Irish artist is speaking to me from an unusual studio space: a farm in Stradbally, County Laois. It may have the odd equine intruder, hungry for press coverage, but it also boasts huge

stories.title: VW faces protests in Germany over proposed job cuts and factory closures
stories.summary: Demonstrations at 18 sites set up as radical transformation plan put to board of Europe’s biggest carmaker Volkswagen’s proposal to slash up to 100,000 jobs and close factories faces a major test on Thursday as formal proposals are put to the supervisory board at Europe’s biggest carmaker, with protests planned at all plants in Germany. IG Metall, the influential staff union, has organised demonstrations involving shop stewards and union council members at 18 sites, including the company’s headq

stories.title: Judge orders $5.8M be paid to E. Jean Carroll in Trump sex abuse case
stories.summary: The money has been held in escrow since 2023 when a jury found Trump had sexually abused and defamed the former Elle magazine columnist. The judge said Trump had been stalling the case and that it was time to pay up.

stories.title: India news: Modi clinches uranium supply deal with Australia
stories.summary: India's nuclear energy ambitions have received a fresh impetus from its Indo-Pacific partner Australia. The two nations have reached a deal to export Australian uranium to India. DW has more.

stories.title: Is the Strait of Hormuz still Iran's trump card?
stories.summary: Iran's latest attacks show it can easily disrupt shipping, energy markets and draw in Gulf neighbors. DW asks how much leverage Tehran really has over Washington and if its high-risk strategy could backfire.

stories.title: DR Congo: Tensions mount over planned constitutional referendum
stories.summary: Tensions are rising in DR Congo over plans to introduce a new constitution. The opposition accuses President Tshisekedi of using it to seek a third term but has postponed planned protests following an AU mediation offer.

stories.title: Fact check: No, Iran did not strike a secret US Gulf base
stories.summary: With President Trump declaring that the ceasefire is "over" after fresh strikes by Iran, a video goes viral on social media allegedly depicting an attack on a US facility by Iran. The video shows something different.

stories.title: East German doping victims still lack adequate support
stories.summary: Thousands of athletes in former East Germany were subjected to state-sponsored doping — and many still suffer lasting health effects. But support for victims is not enough, German commissioner says.

stories.title: Trump's support for Venezuela opposition head Machado wanes
stories.summary: Venezuelan opposition leader and Nobel Peace laureate María Corina Machado says she wants to return to her earthquake-ravaged homeland. Yet neither Venezuela's government nor the United States thinks that's a good idea.

stories.title: Iran war: Trump says ceasefire 'over' after fresh strikes

stories.summary: Russian athletes looking to return to international competition will now be able to compete as long as they "meet relevant anti-doping requirements," the IOC said.

stories.title: New video shows man believed to be Tyler Robinson before Charlie Kirk shooting
stories.summary: The footage shown during the second day of a preliminary hearing appears to show Robinson walking through a parking garage and running across a roof.

stories.title: Argentina fans sing and dance after dramatic World Cup comeback against Egypt
stories.summary: Argentina fans erupted in celebration after the defending champions came from two goals down to beat Egypt 3-2 and reach the World Cup quarter-finals.

stories.title: Nairobi man shares home with rescued birds
stories.summary: Nairobi's 'Birdman' Rodgers Magutha rescues injured birds and lives with them, while defending his unusual bond with wildlife and conservation work.

stories.title: Fears of a massacre in this city on the front line of Sudan's war
stories.summary: El-Obeid has been under "siege-like conditions" for 18 months says the UN human rights chief.

stories.title: 'The water just came so fast': Typhoon triggers floods and rare tornadoes in China
stories.summary: Villagers whose families have been stranded told the BBC they are struggling to get food and help.

stories.title: Top Democrats press Maine Senate candidate to drop out of race over sexual assault allegation
stories.summary: Graham Platner denied the allegation, calling it "categorically false" but said he was "taking the time to reflect on the best path forward".

stories.title: Utah boarding school where Paris Hilton alleged abuse as a teen loses its licence
stories.summary: Hilton said the facility "failed the children", as regulators cited neglect and use of unnecessary restraints.

stories.title: Suspect ate Chick-fil-A and interacted with Charlie Kirk staff before shooting, court hears
stories.summary: The preliminary hearing will determine whether Tyler Robinson, accused of killing conservative activist Charlie Kirk, will stand trial.

stories.title: Trump says ceasefire with Tehran 'over' after US and Iran exchange fire
stories.summary: Washington initiated strikes on Iran overnight in response to cargo vessels being hit in the Strait of Hormuz. Tehran then launched its own attack targeting US bases in the Gulf.

stories.title: Ali Khamenei's coffin passes through thousands of mourners in Iraq's holy city of Najaf
stories.summary: Funeral procession for Ali Khamenei passes through Najaf as thousands of mourners gather, touching the funeral truck and honouring the late leader.

stories.title: Trains and emergency calls affected after major outage at Australia's largest telecoms company
stories.summary: Servers at data centres in Sydney and Melbourne were to blame but the exact cause remains unknown.

stories.title: Erdogan gives world leaders a ceremonial welcome ahead of NATO summit
stories.summary: NATO leaders including Trump and Zelenskyy arrive in Ankara for a state dinner hosted by Erdogan ahead of key summit talks in Turkey.

stories.title: Cuban zoo celebrates birth of Bengal tigers amid energy crisis
stories.summary: Arrival of endangered cats, including rare white cub, revitalises team straining under fuel and medicine shortages For the Cuban zookeeper Ángel Cordero, the sight of four Bengal ⁠tiger cubs playing in a cage at the Cuban national zoo is a small miracle on an island stifled by shortages ⁠of fuel, medicine and ⁠days-long power outages. The ​birth of these endangered big cats – including an exceedingly rare white tiger – has revitalised a team of zoo workers, he said. Continue reading...

stories.title: Canada to buy 12 hi-tech German submarines after bidding war
stories.summary: TKMS beats South Korean rival to multibillion-dollar contract that will deepen Canada’s Nato ties Canada has selected a German consortium to build a dozen cutting-edge submarines in one of the country’s largest-ever defence contracts that will further deepen its Nato ties before a crucial summit this week. On Monday the prime minister, Mark Carney, announced the winner of a tightly contested battle for the lucrative government contract to replace its fleet of ageing, secondhand subs, most of whi

stories.title: ‘Living like this is agony’: Cuba suffers third nationwide blackout in six months
stories.summary: Impoverished island was already struggling to keep the lights on before the US imposed a blockade in January Cuba on Monday suffered its third nationwide power outage since the start of the year, the state electricity company said. The impoverished island was already struggling to keep the lights on before the US president, Donald Trump, imposed an oil blockade in January, which has depleted the already dwindling supply of fuel for Cuba’s power plants. Continue reading...

stories.title: Sri Lanka prison riot kills 26, with more than 100 others wounded
stories.summary: Victims with cuts and gunshot injuries rushed to hospital after fighting between prisoners from two drug gangs Clashes at a Sri Lankan jail have killed 26 people, including seven guards, and wounded more than 100 in the country’s deadliest prison riot in years, officials said. Victims with cuts and gunshot injuries were rushed to Negombo hospital, north of the capital Colombo after overnight fighting between prisoners from two drug gangs, police said on Monday. Continue reading...

stories.title: Five charged in Liberia after more than 200kg of cocaine seized in drug bust
stories.summary: Shipment discovered at airport in Monrovia and valued at £14.2m had been falsely declared as seasoning cubes Authorities in Liberia have charged five suspects over one of the largest drug seizures in the country’s history, after police found more than 200kg of cocaine falsely declared as Maggi seasoning cubes. The shipment, with an estimated value of $19m (£14.2m), was discovered at the international airport in Monrovia on 8 June, but the suspects were not named until a press briefing at the wee

stories.title: Why migrants come to Germany for work and then leave again
stories.summary: Germany has long attracted skilled workers to its labor market. The country has been much less successful at retaining them. So what is Germany doing wrong?

stories.title: At least 26 killed and more than 100 wounded in Sri Lanka prison riot, officials say
stories.summary: The wounded were rushed to the Negombo hospital, north of the capital Colombo, following overnight clashes between inmates from two drug gangs, police said.

stories.title: Oil tanker hit by 'unknown projectile' in Strait of Hormuz, British military says
stories.summary: Iranian state television said a liquified natural gas tanker came under attack Tuesday in the Strait of Hormuz off Oman after ignoring warnings, implying Tehran carried out the assault on a tanker it said was carrying natural gas from Qatar.

stories.title: Argentina fans gather in Atlanta for traditional World Cup 'banderazo'
stories.summary: Hundreds of Argentina supporters gathered in Atlanta for a traditional banderazo rally ahead of their World Cup last-16 match against Egypt.

stories.title: Typhoon Maysak kills two and forces thousands to evacuate in China
stories.summary: Tropical storm causes extreme flooding in south of the country with heavy rainfall expected in coming days A tropical storm has killed two people, caused dam breaches and forced tens of thousands to evacuate in southern China. Typhoon Maysak killed two people in Nanning, in China’s southern Guangxi province. Maysak, which lashed Vietnam and China’s southern island province of Hainan over the weekend, will dump the water it sucked up on its way ⁠across the South China Sea as it weakens and heads

stories.title: Dowry murders in India no longer spark public anger or debate, study finds
stories.summary: Thousands of women are killed in dowry disputes each year, despite the practice being banned in 1961 Dowry deaths in India no longer provoke the public anger they once did, despite thousands of women’s lives still being lost every year, according to new research. The killings – women who are murdered or driven to suicide following dowry disputes between families – have also faded from political debate, despite an increase in cases. Continue reading...

stories.title: Severe storms in China bring tornadoes and landslides that have killed 15 people
stories.summary: Chinese leader Xi Jinping calls for ‘all out’ rescue effort as death toll rises and 16 people ⁠remain buried after a ⁠landslide in the country’s west The death toll from devastating storms in parts of China rose to 15 on Tuesday, with hundreds more injured and tens of thousands evacuated, state media reported, as the country’s leader, Xi Jinping, urged “all out” efforts to rescue people affected by the weather. Thunderstorms and gale-force winds killed at least 11 people and injured 331 in the c

stories.title: Wildfires in southern Europe wreak havoc – in pictures
stories.summary: Wildfires raged across southern Europe, forcing thousands of people to evacuate their homes and prompting officials to ban spectators from a stage of the storied Tour de France cycling race Continue reading...

stories.title: Firefighters battle wildfires across southern Europe
stories.summary: Wildfires are raging across several parts of southern Europe. They come on the heels of massive heat waves in much of the region.

stories.title: Austrian court convicts 2 former Syrian officials of abuse
stories.summary: A Vienna court has convicted two former Syrian security officials, including the former intelligence chief in Raqqa, for offenses including torture and sexual assault against opponents of Bashar Assad's regime.

stories.title: Emmanuel Macron visits Syria, 18 months after Assad's fall
stories.summary: The French president landed in Damascus on Monday evening. He's the first EU leader to visit the country since Bashar Assad's sudden downfall and the rise of former militant turned President Ahmed al-Sharaa.

stories.title: Canada awards Germany major submarine contract
stories.summary: Canadian PM Carney said the deal of up to 12 German-built submarines will be the "largest in Canadian history." Germany had pushed hard for the order, which comes as Canada seeks stronger ties with Europe.

stories.title: How Volkswagen's huge workforce became a liability
stories.summary: The struggling German carmaker plans to cut up to 100,000 jobs from its 630,000-strong workforce. How did VW's headcount get so bloated, and will the cuts be enough to survive the Chinese EV onslaught?
